container.intro: Complete a 100-hour NATCEP program, pass your background check, and pass the state exam to join the Texas Nurse Aide Registry.

hero.title: Becoming a CNA in Texas
hero.dek: Learn Texas HHSC requirements, find a 100-hour NATCEP program, and prepare for the state competency exam.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Texas | Full Requirements & Steps
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to getting your CNA license in Texas. Learn about NATCEP training hours, DPS background check, and the state competency exam.
target_queries: how to become a cna in texas

becoming.faq_heading: Common Questions about Getting a CNA in Texas

sections.label: Basic Requirements
sections.body: To enroll in a Texas NATCEP program, you must be 18 years old and pass a DPS criminal background check.

faq.q: How many hours of training do I need to be a CNA in Texas?
faq.a: Texas requires 100 hours of training: 60 hours of classroom instruction and 40 hours of clinical practice in a state-approved NATCEP.

hero.title: Texas CNA State Exam Application
hero.dek: Apply to take the Texas Nurse Aide written and skills competency exam.

seo.title: Texas CNA Exam Application & Registry Guide
seo.description: How to apply for the Texas CNA state exam and get listed on the Nurse Aide Registry after finishing your NATCEP.

sections.label: Application Steps
sections.body: After completing your training, you must pass the competency exam within 24 months to be added to the registry.

faq.q: How long do I have to take the exam after CNA classes in Texas?
faq.a: You must pass the state exam within 24 months of completing your state-approved training program.

hero.title: Transfer a CNA License to Texas
hero.dek: Texas allows out-of-state CNAs to transfer via Reciprocity if they hold an active certificate and pass a DPS background check.

seo.title: Transfer CNA License to Texas | Reciprocity Steps
seo.description: Learn how to transfer your active out-of-state CNA license to the Texas Nurse Aide Registry via reciprocity form 5505-NAR using the TULIP system.

transfer.faq_heading: Questions about Transferring your CNA to Texas

sections.label: Transfer Requirements
sections.body: Submit Form 5505-NAR through TULIP, complete a DPS fingerprint background check, and show an active out-of-state certification in good standing.

faq.q: How do I transfer my CNA to Texas?
faq.a: You must apply for reciprocity using Form 5505-NAR, submit proof of active certification, and pass a Texas DPS fingerprint background check.
